vulnerability.reference: Low-income applicants self-select out due to perceived rejection, biasing data.
vulnerability.generated: Automated underwriting systems lack selection-bias correction, misinterpreting non-strategic non-participation as low creditworthiness.

impact.reference: Creditworthy non-strategic individuals denied loans, widening wealth gap.
impact.generated: Creditworthy non-strategic applicants are denied loans, entrenching financial exclusion and systemic inequality.

dose_response.reference: Unmodeled selection bias under strategic self-selection enables unfair, uncalibrated credit decisions, which in automated loan approvals exposes loan applicants to inaccurate assessments, leading to denial of creditworthy non-strategic individuals and financial exclusion.
dose_response.generated: Learning under strategic self-selection without modeling selection bias, through loan applicants interacting with automated underwriting, triggers systems to misinterpret non-strategic non-participation as low creditworthiness, leading to creditworthy non-strategic applicants being denied loans and entrenching financial exclusion and systemic inequality.
